ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sections" (
  "section_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each section */,
  "section_name" TEXT /* Name of the section (e.g., [charconv.to.chars]) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the section (e.g., Fix integral conformance) */,
  "link" TEXT /* Link to the section documentation */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the section was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the section was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("section_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tasks" (
  "task_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each task */,
  "section_id" INTEGER /* ID of the section the task belongs to */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the task (e.g., Fix integral conformance) */,
  "dependencies" TEXT /* Dependencies of the task (e.g., None) */,
  "assignee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the assignee */,
  "patch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patch */,
  "status_id" INTEGER /* ID of the status */,
  "first_released_version" TEXT /* First released version of the task (e.g., Clang 13) */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority of the task (e.g., High, Medium, Low) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the task was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the task was last updated */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date when the task started */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date when the task ended */,
  "estimated_time" INTEGER /* Estimated time required for the task */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("task_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tasks_section_id FOREIGN KEY ("section_id") REFERENCES sections ("section_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tasks_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("status_id") REFERENCES status ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tasks_patch_id FOREIGN KEY ("patch_id") REFERENCES patches ("patch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tasks_assignee_id FOREIGN KEY ("assignee_id") REFERENCES assignees ("assignee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "assignees" (
  "assignee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each assignee */,
  "assignee_name" TEXT /* Full name of the assignee */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the assignee */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the assignee (e.g., Developer, Project Manager) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patches" (
  "patch_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patch */,
  "patch_number" TEXT /* Number of the patch (e.g., D100722) */,
  "link" TEXT /* Link to the patch documentation */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the patch */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patch_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the status (e.g., Complete, In Progress) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "task_comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "task_id" INTEGER /* ID of the task */,
  "assignee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the assignee who made the comment */,
  "comment" TEXT /* The comment itself */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the comment was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_task_comments_task_id FOREIGN KEY ("task_id") REFERENCES tasks ("task_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_task_comments_assignee_id FOREIGN KEY ("assignee_id") REFERENCES assignees ("assignee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "task_attachments" (
  "attachment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attachment */,
  "task_id" INTEGER /* ID of the task */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the file */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of the file (e.g., image/jpeg) */,
  "file_size" INTEGER /* Size of the file in bytes */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the attachment was added */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attachment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_task_attachments_task_id FOREIGN KEY ("task_id") REFERENCES tasks ("task_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., Developer, Project Manager) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "assignee_roles" (
  "assignee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the assignee */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignee_id", "role_id")
)
